text: The formation mechanism of carbon dots (CDs) remains unclear. Here, nitrogen-doped carbon dots (NCDs) are fabricated via hydrothermal reaction of 4-aminoantipyrine with the yields of blue emitting nanoparticles ( ~ 5 nm) with high water solubility and quantity in gram scale. By analyzing FT-IR, $^{13}$C-NMR, $^1$H-NMR and UV–visible spectra at different hydrothermal reaction time points of 3, 9 and 21 h in details, the structural evolution and growth mechanism of precursor toward as-prepared NCDs were thoroughly discussed. Spectroscopic results clearly demonstrate that the primary structure of precursor was decomposed to yield new spectral signals attributed to aliphatic and carbonyl functional groups through hydrolyzing the pyrazole ring of precursor and breaking down the noncovalent $\pi$–$\pi$ stacking interactions between aromatic rings for forming CDs core. Meanwhile, the increased surface functional groups with hydrothermal reaction time significantly improved the photoluminescent quantum yield (PLQY) of CDs. The HR-TEM and XRD results demonstrate that high purity of as-fabricated NCDs were with amorphous turbostratic carbon structure and lattice distance of 0.26 nm. Overall, understanding the structural evolution and growth mechanism of precursor toward the CDs and affecting factors of different reaction time, present a facile method to fabricate high quality CDs using nitrogen rich carbon precursor.
